delegation_context.instructions: Create a detailed, spec-aware execution plan for ONLY Phase 4: Iteration — Annotation & Versioning Engine. ADOPT from decisions_so_far: Phase 1 thin-spine schema (requirement_comments with quoted_text+section_hint+state(open|resolved|orphaned)+author_kind, comment_events append-only, requirement_versions+content_hash; NO anchor column), the service DB pattern (flat functions, db_path injectable, get_connection(db_path), like goal_service); Phase 3a's selectable-DOM contract (every block one text-selectable unit, NO id=/data-block-anchor — capture quote + nearest heading at selection). Honor the LOCKED owner decisions from plan.collab.md: #1 thin spine + Claude re-anchor-by-quote (NO deterministic anchoring engine), #7 commenting UX (select text -> 💬 pill -> inline composer anchored to selection, below-default/flip-up-when-cramped, store quoted text, <mark> highlight, human OR agent same path, ~90 lines vanilla JS over Jinja+HTMX, NO framework/annotation lib), #8 version-diff UX (toggle Current vN / Changes since v(N-1); What-changed summary panel + inline tracked-changes green/amber/red; block-level structural set arithmetic over two parsed snapshots, matched by heading token+content; deterministic change SET, subagent may narrate not invent), #9 re-anchor validated during build (trust+iterate; orphaning always surfaced for triage, never silent loss). Plan concretely: (1) comment_service.py BEFORE any UI (FR-013 forcing function): POST/GET .../comments, POST .../comments/{id}/resolve, content-negotiated on HX-Request (JSON for agents, HTML fragment for HTMX) — pattern from api_agents.py; author_kind is the ONLY human/agent distinction, no privileged UI write path. (2) Store comments by quote, re-locate by a Claude re-anchor SUBAGENT (dispatch_mode=subagent, following the cast-goal-classifier/cast-requirements-checker precedent); unfindable quote -> orphaned, surfaced. (3) ~150-line vanilla-JS comment layer over the selectable DOM (selection -> capture quote+heading -> popover -> hx-post), wired on htmx:afterSwap. (4) Editable textarea + re-anchor on save. (5) Version snapshot create_next() gating on open-comment count (open => unconverged), snapshot to new version+content hash, prior archived, comments carry forward + re-located, only current version's files in goal folder (FR-011). (6) Change summary (FR-017): diff parsed blocks of two version snapshots (added/removed/modified/unchanged, matched by heading+content) — structural diff is source of truth, subagent narrates only. **BUILD THIS DIFF ENGINE REUSABLE — Phase 5 consumes the SAME engine; name it clearly and put it where Phase 5 can import it.** (7) Archive retrieval (US5 S3) returns archived version WITH its comments + resolution state (the comment_events trail makes this free). Verification: dual-assertion agent-parity test (one handler returns JSON header-less, HTML fragment on HX-Request); comment re-located after unrelated edit, orphaned when quote genuinely gone; open comment => unconverged, next version => converged; find cast-server -name package.json stays empty. Output ONE plan file to docs/plan/. The reusable diff-engine module name/interface is the key cross-phase contract Phase 5 will adopt — be explicit. Note: cast-detailed-plan may auto-dispatch cast-plan-review (now scope-question-suppressed for delegated runs).

context.goal_title: Refine Requirements v2 — Workflow-Aware, HTML-First Requirements Refinement
context.prior_output: Prior sub-phase decisions are summarized at docs/plan/refine-requirements-v2-decisions-so-far.md. ADOPT their module names, table/column names, function signatures, and the three new agent names unless you have a strong reason to deviate; if you must deviate, document it under a 'Suggested Revisions to Prior Sub-Phases' section.
context.subphase_section:
## Phase 4: Iteration — Annotation & Versioning Engine
**Outcome:** Reviewers (human or agent) leave **block-anchored** comments with an open/resolved
lifecycle and retained trail; unresolved comments mark the spec unconverged and drive new versions;
each new version emits a deterministic **block-level change summary**; only the current version lives
in the goal folder, older versions archived in the DB with comments intact. Built **API-first** so an
agent uses the same door as the human UI.
**Dependencies:** Phase 1 (thin spine + version/comment schema) + Phase 3a (selectable DOM to capture
quotes from).
**Estimated effort:** 3-4 sessions
**Verification:** A single dual-assertion test proves **agent parity** (one handler returns JSON to a
header-less call, HTML fragment to an `HX-Request` call). A comment left on a quote in v2 is re-located
to the right place after an unrelated edit by the re-anchor subagent, and becomes `orphaned` (not lost)
when its quote genuinely no longer exists. A spec with any open comment reports `unconverged`; the next
version flips it to `converged`. `find cast-server -name package.json` stays empty (no framework added).

Key activities:
- **Build the comment API *before any UI*** (the FR-013 forcing function): `comment_service.py` +
  `POST/GET …/comments`, `POST …/comments/{id}/resolve`, content-negotiated on `HX-Request` (JSON for
  agents, HTML fragment for HTMX) — the exact pattern already in `api_agents.py`. `author_kind` is the
  *only* human-vs-agent distinction; **no privileged UI write path.**
- **Store comments by quote, re-locate by subagent** (the thin-spine decision): a comment row holds the
  `quoted_text` + `section_hint` + `version` it was left against — **no stored block anchor.** On
  display against a changed file, a **Claude re-anchor subagent** finds where each open comment now
  belongs from its quote; an unfindable quote → `orphaned`, surfaced for triage (never silently lost).
  This is the deliberate trade: less determinism, far less machinery, intelligence handles drift.
- **Ship a ~150-line vanilla-JS comment layer** over the selectable DOM (selection → capture quote +
  nearest heading → popover → `hx-post`), wired on `htmx:afterSwap`. **No React, no annotation
  library.**
- **Keep the editable textarea + re-anchor on save** (resolves the human-edit-model question): a human
  whole-file edit is allowed; on save, the re-anchor subagent re-locates open comments against the new
  text. No need to forbid free-text editing — the subagent absorbs the drift.
- **Version snapshot + comment carry-over:** `create_next()` gates on open-comment count (open ⇒
  unconverged), snapshots the current file to a new `version` (with its content hash), marks the prior
  archived. Open comments carry forward and are re-located by quote; unfindable → `orphaned`. Only the
  current version's files land in the goal folder (FR-011).
- **Change summary (FR-017):** diff the parsed blocks of two version snapshots
  (`added/removed/modified/unchanged`, matched by heading + content) — the structural diff is the
  source of truth; a Claude subagent may *narrate* it into prose but never *invent* it. **Build this
  reusable — Phase 5 consumes the same engine.**
- **Archive retrieval (US5 S3):** an archived version returns *with* its comments and resolution state
  (the append-only `comment_events` trail makes this free).

---
context.dependencies: Phase 1 (thin spine + version/comment schema) + Phase 3a (selectable DOM to capture quotes from).
